doctrine: Glyphs mark governance events. Primitives name structural behavior.
A slot can be reserved without being authoritative.
A glyph becomes usable by surviving its own misuse cases.
The glyphs do not add meaning. They govern when meaning may be claimed.
A glyph slot is infrastructure. A glyph meaning is authority.

mvs_binding.note: Each glyph activates at a specific MVS step. See GLYPH_MVS_BINDING_TABLE.md.

fidelity_interaction.G2_without_receipt: DAVAR ceiling regardless of other completeness
fidelity_interaction.G2_with_receipt: EMET eligible only after GLYPH-REG-001 receipt + specimen passport

glyphs.anchor_note: Multi-primitive by design. Threshold events produced by T1,T3,R1-D,L2,D1 in bifurcating configurations.
glyphs.function: Marks a threshold, decision, pivot, or bifurcation point.
glyphs.gate_condition: At least two possible governed outputs exist.

nearest_confusions.name: Inhabited Threshold
nearest_confusions.why_not: ◇ marks the gate or event; ◈ marks sustained residence inside threshold pressure.

glyphs.anchor_note: M3 and ◈ are independent decisions. Confirming M3 does not confirm ◈ anchor.
glyphs.function: Marks a threshold condition sustained or occupied over time rather than merely encountered as a momentary event.
glyphs.gate_condition: Threshold condition + duration evidence + frameDeclaration + notAllowedToProve.
glyphs.specimen_gate: PTL-001 proposed; sealed only after P4 frame declaration rule is implemented.

nearest_confusions.name: Threshold Event
nearest_confusions.why_not: ◇ is momentary; ◈ requires sustained duration evidence.

nearest_confusions.name: Liminal Field
nearest_confusions.why_not: M3 names the field condition; ◈ marks it in a scan or receipt.

nearest_confusions.name: Witness Function
nearest_confusions.why_not: P4 witnesses; ◈ marks the threshold condition being witnessed.

nearest_confusions.name: Collapse Basin
nearest_confusions.why_not: T3 is a basin/sink; ◈ is a threshold field and may be non-collapse.

glyphs.anchor_note: ✦ marks when M1 fires at scan entry. G3 and M1 are not identical.
glyphs.function: Marks that a cue has become visible enough to record but not yet classify. Fires at MVS Step 1.
glyphs.gate_condition: Observed cue exists. Primitive assignment has not yet been made.

glyphs.canonical_name_correction_note: Corrected from V original 'Gate Signal' to 'Gate Marker' to disambiguate from M2 primitive 'Gate Signal'.
glyphs.anchor_note: M2 'Gate Signal' = primitive (structural condition). G4 'Gate Marker' = glyph (annotation that M2 is active).
glyphs.function: Marks that a routing, eligibility, release, or transition gate controls what may happen next.
glyphs.gate_condition: Named gate condition exists and governs routing.
glyphs.short_rule: ⚑ marks a condition that governs routing.

nearest_confusions.name: Refusal Mark
nearest_confusions.why_not: ⚑ marks a navigable gate; ⊘ marks a hard block.

glyphs.valida_critical_rule: GATE_MARKER_AS_REFUSAL: ⚑ used where ⊘ required. Severity: Critical.
glyphs.promotion_blocker: must not become confused with refusal

glyphs.anchor_note: Intentionally unanchored. ⊘ marks a cross-primitive governance event. No single primitive owns refusal. D-003 closed by this design decision.
glyphs.function: Marks an invalid transition, blocked claim, rejected overclaim, or VALIDA refusal.
glyphs.gate_condition: A named rule explicitly blocks the claim, transition, or render request.

nearest_confusions.name: Gate Marker
nearest_confusions.why_not: ⚑ = navigable gate; ⊘ = hard block requiring rule_id.

glyphs.anchor_note: Unanchored by design. Confusion marks arise across all category transitions.
glyphs.function: Marks that a nearest-confusion check is active or required.
glyphs.gate_condition: Primary assignment has a plausible adjacent alternative that must be named and rejected.
glyphs.promotion_value: Highest-priority glyph to promote after ◈. Operationalizes FLOOR-004.

glyphs.anchor_note: Unanchored. Receipt marks arise at output layer of any governed scan, patch, or decision. EMIT-coupled.
glyphs.function: Marks that a scan, patch, decision, release, or disagreement has produced a receipt-bearing record.
glyphs.gate_condition: Timestamp + registry hash + allowedToProve + notAllowedToProve are present.
glyphs.promotion_blocker: must not be treated as truth or validation

transition_authority.stages_0_to_2: Thread A or Thread B with documentation

glyph_state_machine.demotion_rule: Any safety failure or governance breach can demote to reserved_candidate or superseded
